question:
I'm new to this codebase and need a real code-based tour before my first behavior change.

Find the central runtime flow, trace it from the public API to the code that does the work, and show the nearby code paths and tests I should read next.

graph.answer:
**Runtime Flow**
- The public type is `nlohmann::json`, which is just `basic_json<>` in [json_fwd.hpp](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/include/nlohmann/json_fwd.hpp#L62). The concrete implementation and almost all user-facing APIs live in [json.hpp](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/include/nlohmann/json.hpp#L127).
- Parse entrypoint: `json::parse(...)` in [json.hpp](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/include/nlohmann/json.hpp#L4090) turns the input into a `detail::input_adapter` and hands it to `detail::parser`.
- The actual text parsing work happens in [parser.hpp](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/include/nlohmann/detail/input/parser.hpp#L95) and especially the recursive descent loop in [parser.hpp](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/include/nlohmann/detail/input/parser.hpp#L182). The adapters that feed it are in [input_adapters.hpp](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/include/nlohmann/detail/input/input_adapters.hpp#L495).
- The stream deserialization operator follows the same path in [json.hpp](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/include/nlohmann/json.hpp#L4227).
- Dump entrypoint: `json::dump(...)` in [json.hpp](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/include/nlohmann/json.hpp#L1338) constructs `detail::serializer` and delegates to [serializer.hpp](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/include/nlohmann/detail/output/serializer.hpp#L107).
- The serializer’s core branching is in [serializer.hpp](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/include/nlohmann/detail/output/serializer.hpp#L107); strings recurse through [serializer.hpp](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/include/nlohmann/detail/output/serializer.hpp#L391), integers through [serializer.hpp](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/include/nlohmann/detail/output/serializer.hpp#L714), and floats through [serializer.hpp](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/include/nlohmann/detail/output/serializer.hpp#L799).
- `operator<<` uses the same serializer in [json.hpp](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/include/nlohmann/json.hpp#L4050).
- Binary formats are routed from [json.hpp](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/include/nlohmann/json.hpp#L4348) through `binary_writer` / `binary_reader`, with format-specific `to_*` and `from_*` helpers.

**What To Read Next**
- Parser behavior, callbacks, strictness, and accept-vs-parse coverage: [tests/src/unit-class_parser.cpp](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/tests/src/unit-class_parser.cpp#L349)
- Position-sensitive parse diagnostics and “last read” tracking: [tests/src/unit-class_parser_diagnostic_positions.cpp](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/tests/src/unit-class_parser_diagnostic_positions.cpp#L354)
- Dump formatting, UTF-8 handling, stream operators, and binary-value rendering: [tests/src/unit-serialization.cpp](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/tests/src/unit-serialization.cpp#L18)
- Input adapters, BOM handling, contiguous containers, streams, and malformed-input behavior: [tests/src/unit-deserialization.cpp](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/tests/src/unit-deserialization.cpp#L237)
- Canonical README-style usage examples for parse/dump/from-stream flows: [tests/src/unit-readme.cpp](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/json/tests/src/unit-readme.cpp#L115)
- If you touch binary formats, also read the format-specific tests in `tests/src/unit-cbor.cpp`, `tests/src/unit-msgpack.cpp`, `tests/src/unit-ubjson.cpp`, `tests/src/unit-bson.cpp`, and `tests/src/unit-bjdata.cpp`.

I also wrote the project memories for future reference; from the repo root you can run `serena memories check` to validate them.

If you want, I can next do one of these:
1. Trace a specific behavior slice end-to-end, like comments, trailing commas, BOM handling, or diagnostics.
2. Give you a “first edit” map for the safest place to change parse or dump behavior without breaking compatibility.
